<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)]][[en:Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)]][[ja:牛塚古墳 (壬生町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17989167]] Wikidata name: Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ushiuzuka Kofun | native_name =牛塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ushizuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ushizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ushiuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.437306|N|139.807667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ushizuka Kofun'''|牛塚古墳 |}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/475 |title=牛塚古墳|trans-title=Atagozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ushizuka Kofun is located on the south bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the center of Mibu Town. It is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kurumazuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)|Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Kuramazuka Kofun, the largest in Tochigi Prefecture, is adjacent to the east, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 450 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a [[:en:Scallop|Scallop]] shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Mibu Station|Mibu Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.tochigiji.or.jp/spot/5058/ Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341009.htm Mibu Department of Education]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)]][[en:Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)]][[ja:牛塚古墳 (壬生町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17989167]] Wikidata name: Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ushiuzuka Kofun | native_name =牛塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ushizuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ushizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ushiuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.437306|N|139.807667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ushizuka Kofun'''|牛塚古墳 |}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/475 |title=牛塚古墳|trans-title=Atagozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ushizuka Kofun is located on the south bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the center of Mibu Town. It is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kurumazuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)|Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Kuramazuka Kofun, the largest in Tochigi Prefecture, is adjacent to the east, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 450 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a [[:en:Scallop|Scallop]] shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Mibu Station|Mibu Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.tochigiji.or.jp/spot/5058/ Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341009.htm Mibu Department of Education]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)]][[en:Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)]][[ja:牛塚古墳 (壬生町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17989167]] Wikidata name: Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ushiuzuka Kofun | native_name =牛塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ushizuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ushizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ushiuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.437306|N|139.807667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ushizuka Kofun'''|牛塚古墳 |}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/475 |title=牛塚古墳|trans-title=Atagozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ushizuka Kofun is located on the south bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the center of Mibu Town. It is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kurumazuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)|Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Kuramazuka Kofun, the largest in Tochigi Prefecture, is adjacent to the east, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 450 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a [[:en:Scallop|Scallop]] shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Mibu Station|Mibu Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.tochigiji.or.jp/spot/5058/ Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341009.htm Mibu Department of Education]

The Kuramazuka Kofun, the largest in Tochigi Prefecture, is adjacent to the east, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 450 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)]][[en:Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu)]][[ja:牛塚古墳 (壬生町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17989167]] Wikidata name: Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ushiuzuka Kofun | native_name =牛塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ushizuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ushizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ushiuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.437306|N|139.807667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ushizuka Kofun'''|牛塚古墳 |}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/475 |title=牛塚古墳|trans-title=Atagozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ushizuka Kofun is located on the south bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the center of Mibu Town. It is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kurumazuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)|Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Kuramazuka Kofun, the largest in Tochigi Prefecture, is adjacent to the east, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 450 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a [[:en:Scallop|Scallop]] shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Mibu Station|Mibu Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

entry [[d:Q17989167]] Wikidata name: Ushizuka Kofun (Mibu) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ushiuzuka Kofun | native_name =牛塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ushizuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ushizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ushiuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.437306|N|139.807667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ushizuka Kofun'''|牛塚古墳 |}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/475 |title=牛塚古墳|trans-title=Atagozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ushizuka Kofun is located on the south bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the center of Mibu Town. It is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kurumazuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)|Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Kuramazuka Kofun, the largest in Tochigi Prefecture, is adjacent to the east, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 450 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a [[:en:Scallop|Scallop]] shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Mibu Station|Mibu Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

| native_name_lang = | image = Ushizuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ushizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ushiuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.437306|N|139.807667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ushizuka Kofun'''|牛塚古墳 |}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of [[:en:Mibu, Tochigi|Mibu]], [[:en:Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|Shimotsuga District]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/475 |title=牛塚古墳|trans-title=Atagozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ushizuka Kofun is located on the south bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the center of Mibu Town. It is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kurumazuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)|Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Kuramazuka Kofun, the largest in Tochigi Prefecture, is adjacent to the east, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 450 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a [[:en:Scallop|Scallop]] shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke area]] was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji]] indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Mibu Station|Mibu Station]] on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] [[:en:Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a {{ill|Scallop|en|Scallop|WD=Q1311395|lt=Scallop}} shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke area}} was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|en|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|WD=Q11361381|lt=Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji}} indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Mibu Station|en|Mibu Station|WD=Q6827788|lt=Mibu Station}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] {{ill|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|en|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q3449834|lt=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ushiuzuka Kofun | native_name =牛塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ushizuka-kofun (Mibu)-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ushizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ushiuzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.437306|N|139.807667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Mibu, Tochigi|en|Mibu, Tochigi|WD=Q1354910|lt=Mibu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ushizuka Kofun'''|牛塚古墳 |}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of {{ill|Mibu, Tochigi|en|Mibu, Tochigi|WD=Q1354910|lt=Mibu}}, {{ill|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|WD=Q1202834|lt=Shimotsuga District}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/475 |title=牛塚古墳|trans-title=Atagozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ushizuka Kofun is located on the south bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the center of Mibu Town. It is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kurumazuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)|Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Kuramazuka Kofun, the largest in Tochigi Prefecture, is adjacent to the east, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 450 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a {{ill|Scallop|en|Scallop|WD=Q1311395|lt=Scallop}} shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke area}} was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|en|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|WD=Q11361381|lt=Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji}} indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Mibu Station|en|Mibu Station|WD=Q6827788|lt=Mibu Station}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] {{ill|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|en|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q3449834|lt=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.tochigiji.or.jp/spot/5058/ Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341009.htm Mibu Department of Education]

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a {{ill|Scallop|en|Scallop|WD=Q1311395|lt=Scallop|1=Scallop}} shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke area|1=Shimotsuke Province}} was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|en|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|WD=Q11361381|lt=Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|1=Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji}} indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Mibu Station|en|Mibu Station|WD=Q6827788|lt=Mibu Station|1=Mibu Station}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] {{ill|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|en|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q3449834|lt=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|1=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ushizuka Kofun'''|牛塚古墳 |}} is a scallop-shaped [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the town of {{ill|Mibu, Tochigi|en|Mibu, Tochigi|WD=Q1354910|lt=Mibu|1=Mibu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|WD=Q1202834|lt=Shimotsuga District|1=Shimotsuga District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1926.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/475 |title=牛塚古墳|trans-title=Atagozuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ushizuka Kofun is located on the south bank of the Kurokawa River, to the northeast of the center of Mibu Town. It is part of a group of tumuli which include the nearby [[Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kurumazuka Kofun]] and [[Atagozuka Kofun (Mibu)|Atagozuka Kofun]], which have different shapes, and which have separate National Historic Site designations. The Kuramazuka Kofun, the largest in Tochigi Prefecture, is adjacent to the east, while the Atagozuka Kofun is located 450 meters to the south.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=hotategai-gata-kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}, which is shaped like a {{ill|Scallop|en|Scallop|WD=Q1311395|lt=Scallop|1=Scallop}} shell when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two tiers, with the base platform having a total length of 46.5 meters. The base has a circular platform that is 34.5 meters in diameter and a rectangular extension with width of 25 meters, and is five meters high. It is surrounded by a moat. On this platform, the ''kofun'' itself was constructed, with a length of 40 meters circular portion with diameter of 27.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/> It has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but from its design of the mound and from fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' which have been recovered from the vicinity, it is estimated to date from the late-6th century AD. This was a period of transition between the [[Kofun period]] when the {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke area|1=Shimotsuke Province}} was ruled by semi-independent local kings owing fealty to the centralized [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]]. Its size, and location near other large tumuli and the site of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|en|Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|WD=Q11361381|lt=Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|1=Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji}} indicate the importance of this region in Kofun period Shimotsuke.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Ushiuzuka Kofun is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Mibu Station|en|Mibu Station|WD=Q6827788|lt=Mibu Station|1=Mibu Station}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] {{ill|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|en|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|WD=Q3449834|lt=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|1=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
